key_points: Dorms reported as smaller than comparable schools
No major housing scandals surfaced in data
Alumni recall McIntyre Hall and campus living fondly
key_quotes: the dorms look smaller than some other schools I'm looking at and I'm not sure I would be happy in a small space
Lots of great memories with the McIntyre Hall, Washakie Dining Center

key_points: Campus culture is heavily cowboy/country — niche but real
Small-town Laramie limits nightlife and off-campus options significantly
Political debates (Charlie Kirk visit) reveal an ideologically divided campus
key_quotes: believe me NO ONE wants to go to the University of Wyoming
I want to go to a college that has cowboys... hard workin kids who are funny as hell

key_points: Limited food variety — options described as repetitive
Washakie Dining Center is the main hub, nostalgically remembered
Off-campus, Crowbar's Pad Thai Fries singled out as a highlight
key_quotes: I totally agree that the Pad Thai Fries from Crowbar absolutely slaps
I also agree that there are not much food options because they are mostly the same

key_points: Isolation in small-town Laramie is a recurring concern for students
No dedicated mental health support resources mentioned by students
Students transferring out cite unhappiness and disconnection as drivers
key_quotes: I go to UW and it's my second semester and I'm pretty unhappy so far
no one I can talk to, no friend group, just some independent people I'll talk to here and there

key_points: Frequently cited as a true financial safety school for OOS students
WUE and Niche scholarship programs make it affordable for out-of-state
One student received a scholarship via Niche Direct Admissions after rejections elsewhere
key_quotes: my only true financial and admissions safety, my state school, the University of Wyoming
I just found out about niche direct admissions and got a scholarship to the university of wyoming

key_points: Noted as a solid petroleum engineering program nationally
CS and competitive majors have limited data but UW system grading is harsh
Average UW system curve lands students around 2.7 GPA doing average work
key_quotes: University of Wyoming — listed under Petroleum engineering as a relatively unknown but strong program
hitting average in chem, for example, will give you a 2.8 for that class... doing average in all your classes will usually land you ~2.7 overall GPA

key_points: Admissions response time complained about publicly on campus tour video
No systemic admin praise or complaints emerged from student data
Sorority/trans student lawsuit reflects admin policy disputes making national news
key_quotes: I applied since November and we're in mid January. I still have not gotten a feedback. Care to tell me what is going on?
Artimis doesn't live in the sorority house. They live off campus and didn't plan to move into the sorority when this lawsuit was filed

key_points: Laramie is extremely remote — a significant lifestyle adjustment for most
Campus described as beautiful with notable outdoor/skiing access nearby
Wyoming is cold and harsh; wind and altitude catch students off guard
key_quotes: Only thing I splurged on was skiing. At least 12 times per year
Lots of great memories with my walk to classes through Prexy's Pasture everyday

key_points: Petroleum engineering grads have strong industry pipeline from UW
No tech recruiting presence comparable to UW Seattle mentioned
Fashion design alumni have gone on to Parsons/FIT for advanced study
key_quotes: I graduated with a Design, Textiles and Merchandising degree with a minor in Fashion Design in 2015 from the University of Wyoming
University of Wyoming — listed for Petroleum engineering programs

key_points: Cheapest real university option for many OOS students via WUE
In-state tuition with Regents scholarship can mean nearly free attendance
Students use it explicitly as a financial safety when all else fails
key_quotes: my only true financial and admissions safety, my state school, the University of Wyoming
I have a 3.5 and Regents gives me free college tuition

key_points: Best described as a last-resort financial safety or niche fit for outdoorsy, rural types
Students who thrive here love skiing, ranching culture, and small-town life
Those expecting urban energy or competitive peer culture will be disappointed
key_quotes: believe me NO ONE wants to go to the University of Wyoming
I want to go to a college that has cowboys... not rednecks but hard workin kids

key_points: Isolation: Laramie's remoteness causes reported depression and social disconnect
Social life: Students transferring out cite inability to make meaningful friends
Controversy: National lawsuit over trans student in sorority reflects campus tension
key_quotes: I go to UW and it's my second semester and I'm pretty unhappy so far. I've made some friends, but it's nothing like what I had in high school
People like him are why there is trans panic happening in our country. He 100% took advantage of a policy

key_points: Skiing access is exceptional — alumni skied 12+ times per year affordably
Petroleum engineering program is nationally respected and under-publicized
Niche Direct Admissions scholarship offers real money to students locked out elsewhere
key_quotes: Only thing I splurged on was skiing. At least 12 times per year. Otherwise starvation budget
I just found out about niche direct admissions and got a scholarship to the university of wyoming (go cowboys!)
